description: E2E concurrency test: 3 clients submit to the single-GPU service; server processes one at a time (FIFO); client3 polls and pulls its artifacts. Each client first registers (POST /v1/clients {client_id}) to get a secret client_key, sent as the X-Client-Key header to enqueue and to list its own jobs.

register.returns: {client_id, client_key}  (client_key shown once; send as 'X-Client-Key' on every enqueue/list)

client2_bilibili.note: Bilibili (b23.tv short link). Works because the worker uses the logged-in cookies from scripts/bili_login.py (data/bili_cookies.txt / $YT_COOKIES); without them a datacenter IP gets HTTP 412.

long_form.description: Long-form / OOM regression: a video longer than one GPU pass. The SERVER chunks it (~1000s/chunk), transcribes each chunk with the resident model, offsets timestamps, and MERGES into ONE unified transcript — no client-side clipping, no CUDA OOM. Before this fix, a single full pass OOM'd at ~21GB on the 24GB card (the client had to clip to ~5min).
long_form.expect: status -> done (NOT failed/OOM). segments.json has chunked=true, chunk_seconds~1000, last segment end ~1800s (full coverage). Runner: bash benchmark/e2e/run_longform.sh
long_form.full_video: Omit the clip for the full 5069s (84.5min) video -> ~7 chunks, still one merged transcript. Run: bash benchmark/e2e/run_longform.sh http://localhost:39999 ''

note_auth: Every POST /v1/tasks and /v1/tasks/upload carries the header 'X-Client-Key: <client_key>' from registration; without it the enqueue is 401. A client lists its own jobs with GET /v1/tasks + X-Client-Key (sees ONLY its tasks).
then: Each POST returns a per-task pull_token. client3 polls GET /v1/tasks/{id} with header 'X-Task-Token: <pull_token>' (or its X-Client-Key) until done, then GET /v1/tasks/{id}/artifact (same auth) -> zip. /v1/queue is the admin view (bearer-gated when TTS_SERVE_API_KEY set).
